title: R1.1 - Add Markdown preview mode switch
body:
## Objective
Let users switch the right-panel Markdown preview between:
- active stage preview
- full project export preview

## Tasks
- add a small preview mode setting
- keep the existing full-project preview as one mode
- restore a stage-scoped preview builder for the active stage
- wire the preview panel to the selected mode

## Constraints
- preserve the current Markdown export format
- do not redesign the Markdown renderer
- do not add section navigation or collapsible blocks yet

## Validation
- users can switch between stage and full preview
- the full preview still matches the Markdown export
- the stage preview follows the active stage
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert preview mode setting and panel wiring

title: R1.2 - Add nominal workspace path settings
body:
## Objective
Add a lightweight way for users to define their preferred workspace conventions for:
- JSON imports
- JSON exports
- Markdown exports
- attachments

## Tasks
- add settings fields for nominal/preferred paths or folder labels
- surface these values in the Settings screen
- show them in export/import related UI where helpful
- keep values local-only

## Constraints
- do not promise true filesystem write access
- do not introduce browser-specific file APIs beyond current behavior
- keep the feature informational/configurational for now

## Validation
- settings persist across reloads
- preferred workspace values are visible in the UI
- no regression in current import/export flows
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert nominal workspace settings and related UI hints

title: R1.3 - Clarify decision tree destinations and handoff actions
body:
## Objective
Make the decision tree outcomes clearer and more actionable inside the app.

## Tasks
- review existing destination outcomes
- make destination labels and explanations explicit in the UI
- clarify the handoff action for each destination (backlog, reframe, new project, architectural note, technical note)
- keep the current routing minimal and deterministic

## Constraints
- do not redesign the whole modal
- do not change project data structures unless strictly necessary
- preserve current decision-tree logic unless a small fix is required for consistency

## Validation
- each destination has a clear in-app explanation
- each destination has a clear next action
- no regression in existing decision-tree flow
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert decision-tree wording and handoff changes

title: R1.4 - Apply theme setting across the app shell
body:
## Objective
Make the existing `theme` setting actually affect the application consistently.

## Tasks
- define the minimal supported themes (for example dark / light, or current / light)
- connect the stored setting to top-level CSS or document attributes
- ensure major layout areas follow the selected theme
- keep the implementation small and reversible

## Constraints
- do not redesign the product UI
- do not introduce a large theming framework
- keep existing settings compatibility

## Validation
- changing the theme updates the application visibly
- theme choice persists after refresh
- app remains readable in supported themes
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert theme wiring while preserving stored settings

title: R1.5 - Clarify PWA identity and update behavior
body:
## Objective
Reduce ambiguity around install/update behavior for the PWA, especially on mobile.

## Tasks
- review manifest identity fields (`name`, `short_name`, `id`, `start_url`, `scope`)
- document the intended update flow in the repo
- make the in-app update prompt wording explicit if needed
- keep runtime changes minimal and focused

## Constraints
- do not introduce a backend
- do not change unrelated app behavior
- avoid broad PWA refactors unless required by the identity fix

## Validation
- manifest identity is coherent
- update behavior is documented clearly
- prompt/update UX remains functional
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert manifest and prompt wording changes

title: R1.6 - Add export for all projects as one JSON bundle
body:
## Objective
Allow users to export all stored projects in one JSON bundle for backup or transfer.

## Tasks
- add an export-all action in Settings or another appropriate global location
- serialize all projects with a small bundle wrapper or a clearly documented array format
- keep per-project export untouched

## Constraints
- preserve existing single-project export behavior
- keep the format simple and documented
- avoid changing project schema unnecessarily

## Validation
- all current projects can be exported in one file
- single-project export still works
- bundle content is valid JSON
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert the export-all action and serializer

title: R1.7 - Add import for project bundle restore
body:
## Objective
Allow users to restore a previously exported multi-project JSON bundle.

## Tasks
- add an import path for the global project bundle
- validate imported structure safely
- merge or insert imported projects with predictable behavior
- surface simple import errors to the user

## Constraints
- do not silently overwrite projects
- preserve existing single-project import behavior where possible
- keep validation minimal but explicit

## Validation
- a valid bundle restores multiple projects
- invalid bundle structure fails safely
- no silent overwrite occurs
- build passes

## Rollback
- revert bundle import handling
